content.problemStatement:
Chef has a list of intervals intervalListintervalListintervalList, where each interval is represented by a start and an end point.  

Chef wants to remove the minimum number of intervals so that no two remaining intervals overlap. 

Help Chef find the minimum count of intervals that must be removed to achieve this.

Note:An interval is represented as **[start, end]**, where `start < end`.

Two intervals are considered **non-overlapping** if they share **any common point**.
Intervals that touch at endpoints (e.g. `[1,2]` and `[2,3]`) are considered **non-overlapping**.

The task is to remove the **minimum number of intervals** so that no two remaining intervals overlap. This is equivalent to selecting the **maximum number of non-overlapping intervals**, where an interval can be selected only if its `start` is **greater** than or **equal** to the `end` of the previously selected interval.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindMinimumRemovalsfindMinimumRemovalsfindMinimumRemovals — This function determines the minimum number of intervals to remove to eliminate all overlaps in a given list of intervals.

Parameters
intervalListintervalListintervalList: A reference to a vector containing intervals, where each interval is represented as a vector of two integers [start,end][start, end][start,end].
The intervals represent ranges with integer start and end points.
Intervals may overlap or touch at endpoints.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the minimum count of intervals that must be removed to ensure no intervals overlap.
Constraints
1≤intervalList.size()≤1051 \leq \text{intervalList.size()} \leq 10^51≤intervalList.size()≤105
Each interval has exactly two integers: startstartstart and endendend
−5×104≤starti<endi≤5×104-5 \times 10^4 \leq \text{start}_i < \text{end}_i \leq 5 \times 10^4−5×104≤starti​<endi​≤5×104

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of:

The first line contains an integer NNN — the number of intervals Chef has.
The next NNN lines each contain two integers startistart_istarti​ and endiend_iendi​ describing the intervals.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of intervals Chef must remove so that no intervals overlap.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T — the number of test cases. Each test case consists of: The first line contains an integer N — the number of intervals Chef has. The next N lines each contain two integers start_i and end_i describing the intervals.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of intervals Chef must remove so that no intervals overlap.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation: First test case: we have to remove [1 2] as it is overlapping with [0 1] and [3 4].

Second test case: we can choose any one of the intervals only as all of them are overlapping thus we have to remove any 2 intervals.

Third test case: we have to remove [1 4] as it is overlapping the whole interval but by removing it we can have non-overlapping interval: [1 2][2 4].
Explanation: We have to remove [2 4] and [3 5] as they are overlapping with [1 3].
Explanation: There are no overlapping intervals, so we don't need to remove any intervals.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using a greedy algorithm. The idea is to sort the intervals based on their end points and then iterate through the sorted intervals. For each interval, if it overlaps with the previously selected interval, we increment the removal count. Otherwise, we update the end point of the previously selected interval.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total. The sorting step takes O(N log N) time, and the iteration through the sorted intervals takes O(N) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the array of intervals.

code.Python3:
def find_minimum_removals(intervals):
    if not intervals:
        return 0

    # Sort intervals based on end points
    intervals.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])

    removals = 0
    end = intervals[0][1]

    for i in range(1, len(intervals)):
        if intervals[i][0] < end:
            # Overlap: remove interval
            removals += 1
        else:
            # No overlap: keep interval & update
            end = intervals[i][1]

    return removals

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

for _ in range(int(input())):
    n = int(input())
    intervals = [tuple(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(n)]
    print(find_minimum_removals(intervals))
